steps.title: Identify the core traits that drive behavior
steps.content: The opening establishes the passage's central claim: “We are creatures of the sun. We love light and life. That is why we crowd into the towns and cities.” The phrase “creatures of the sun” signals that certain traits—our need for light and human connection—directly determine where people choose to live.

steps.title: Apply the driving traits to the choices
steps.content:
Answer D states the passage's purpose correctly: explaining the common traits that motivate people to choose cities. The other choices either ignore the passage's stated motivation, add tangential elements, or reverse the stated preference.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
